title: Data Analytics with Excel & Power BI
overview: Excel aur Power BI data analytics ke primary pillars hain. Is course mein hum Excel pivot tables se lekar advanced DAX calculations aur real-time Power BI dashboards design karna seekhenge step-by-step.
objectives: Excel data cleaning techniques, key formulas aur pivot tables par grip banana.
Power BI interface, data extraction, validation, aur modeling setup karna.
Advanced DAX modeling (Measures, Columns, Relationships) explore karna.
Indian business use cases (like Retail / E-commerce sales analysis) par complete dashboard build karna.

phases.title: Phase 1: Excel Essentials (Weeks 1-2)
phases.desc: Foundational cleaning formulas, sorting, pivoting aur dashboard designing in Excel.

phases.title: Phase 2: Power BI ETL & Modeling (Weeks 3-5)
phases.desc: Importing multiple data sources, relationship creation, validation, and DAX calculations.

phases.title: Phase 3: Portfolio & Capstone (Week 6)
phases.desc: D-Mart style customer retention analytics dashboard project, resume writing & Upwork guide.

lessons.title: Introduction to Data Analytics & Data Hygiene
lessons.content:
Data cleaning, data analytics ka 80% work hota hai. Jab tak data clean aur hybrid structure mein na ho, models analysis wrong predictions dete hain. Excel primary application hai data hygiene achieve karne ke liye.

### Core Operations:
1. **TRIM**: Remove trailing, leading and redundant spaces.
2. **UPPER / LOWER / PROPER**: Normalise case matching for name variables.
3. **Remove Duplicates**: Data validation panel controls duplicate primary key entries.
lessons.diagram: Raw Database Table -> Trim & Proper Case Conversion -> Unique Primary Keys Table

quiz.question: Excel data mein raw duplicate rows clean karne ke liye primary tool kya hai?
options: Data Tab > Remove Duplicates
Home Tab > Find & Select
Formulas Tab > Clean Output
Insert Tab > Clean Checkbox
quiz.explanation: Data Tab inside ribbon main standard command default data validations and duplicates checking ke liye standard tool data hygiene setup me work karti hai.

assignment.title: Kirana Store customer database cleaning assignment
assignment.prompt: Download standard customer registry list and perform case hygiene formatting, strip leading spaces and extract unique customer IDs using functions.

lessons.title: Retrieving data using VLOOKUP and XLOOKUP
lessons.content:
Data retrieval models mapping process me key lookup arrays implement karna primary query standard hai. 

### VLOOKUP vs XLOOKUP:
- **VLOOKUP**: Limitations like strictly left-to-right search, static column indices (e.g. index column number counts).
- **XLOOKUP**: Native match default parameters, searches left or right dynamically, fallback error support built-in.
lessons.diagram: Customer Lookup ID -> Search in Database column -> Return Matching values dynamic array

quiz.question: XLOOKUP ke inside error handling parameters (if not found parameter) default support use karne ka function style logic?
options: 4th parameter me define nested value string directly write rules
3rd parameter me check validation rules
IFERROR query wrappers nested validation checks only
Formula properties configuration panel overrides standard value definitions
quiz.explanation: XLOOKUP contains a direct 4th parameter called [if_not_found] which enables user fallback configuration natively without extra wrappers.

lessons.title: Understanding syntax rules for Measures and Calculated Columns in DAX
lessons.content: Data Analysis Expressions (DAX) Power BI functions programming framework data model manipulation calculations algorithms setup parameters logic targets standard filters metrics evaluations design maps relationships arrays.
lessons.diagram: DAX Query evaluation model: User Filters -> Row-Context mapping -> Filter-Context execution

quiz.question: Calculated Column and Measure query expressions rules inside DAX model definitions structure differences:
options: Calculated Columns row-by-row memory space allocate checks, Measures evaluate on dynamic visual filter queries
Calculated Columns dynamic rules, Measures static memory allocations
Formula attributes configuration variables definition settings validation query outputs
Data types validations standard parameter calculations rules metrics mappings configurations
quiz.explanation: Calculated Columns allocate static storage values in memory database tables, whereas Measures calculate dynamic values during query execution.

interview_questions.question: Explain Star Schema and SnowFlake Schema differences in Power BI database modeling.
interview_questions.answer: Star Schema is highly optimized for performance where fact tables are linked directly to flat dimension tables. Snowflake Schema contains normalized dimension tables linked recursively to sub-dimensions, causing multi-hop join queries that decrease data refresh speeds.

interview_questions.question: How do you optimize slow DAX queries in a complex dashboard containing over 50 metric cards?
interview_questions.answer: Identify bottlenecks using Performance Analyzer. Replace complex row-level context variables with optimized measures, split visual cards into separate summary pages, pre-calculate index values inside Power Query editor (ETL level) and disable auto-detect date/time parameters.
